meta.title: Yasser El-Sayed M. El-Alfy — Researcher
meta.description: Researcher specializing in 3D SLAM, Depth Estimation, and Road Anomaly Detection.

basics.name: Yasser El-Sayed M. El-Alfy
basics.shortBio: Yasser El-Alfy is a researcher at KFUPM working on learning-based perception and 3D SLAM. His work focuses on integrating deep learning with traditional robotics to build robust navigation systems for autonomous vehicles and drones.
basics.heroSubtitle: Pushing the boundaries of 3D Perception and Autonomous Systems.

strategyOverview.text: My research focuses on bridging the gap between raw sensor data and high-level spatial understanding. I develop monocular depth estimation models and integrate them into SLAM pipelines to enable 3D mapping without expensive LiDAR hardware.

software: Microsoft office programs (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access)

patents.title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR DETECTING AND COUNTING ROAD ANOMALIES
patents.description: A system and a non-transitory computer-readable storage medium for executing a method of detecting road anomalies includes obtaining visual data of a road from an end computing device and inputting the visual data to a machine learning (ML) model that learns to detect and classify at least one road anomaly such as a pothole, longitudinal crack, transverse crack, or alligator crack. The class and a bounding box of at least one road anomaly are output. Multiple post-detection features of the road anomaly, such as an object box area, intersection of neighboring object boxes, union of neighboring object boxes are determined. When the road anomaly is detected across multiple sequential frames, a number of skip frames is determined based on a model fidelity distance (MFD) and a vehicle speed.

talks.title: Multi-Modal Curriculum Federated Learning for Breast Cancer detection
talks.abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most life-threatening cancers worldwide. Early detection is important for improving treatment outcomes and survival rates. Routine examination results are dominated by negative samples which limit the accuracy of the Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) system due to a small number of positive sample representations. Aggregating data from multiple sources is a viable solution to address the limited samples of positive cases. Federated Learning (FL) emerged as a technique to learn features collaboratively from different locations. However, these models are still trained following the traditional diagnostic method separately, limiting the integration and analysis of diverse patient data. To address this, multi-modal federated learning (MMFL) emerged as a promising solution that combines FL and multi-modal learning (MML) to improve diagnostic accuracy while preserving data privacy. In this work, we leveraged MMFL to enhance breast cancer classification by integrating multiple modalities (image and text) from multiple institutions. The text was generated using a caption generator to improve information and enhance the model learning of complex features more effectively. Our approach outperforms single-modality models, achieving better accuracy in fewer training rounds. By facilitating collaboration across institutions and maintaining privacy, MMFL offers a scalable, flexible, and more efficient method for personalized breast cancer diagnosis and treatment planning.
talks.location: Bldg. 22, Room 119, King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals (KFUPM)
